violations.description: General food protection. — ---Box of tomatoes found moldy.--Food item was not protected from potential contamination. -Multiple sauce bins had metal bowls stored in contact with sauce product in walk-in cooler--Potentially hazardous food was not stored at proper temperature of. -Very large cooking pot of green sauce was stored on the floor of the walk cooler at a temperature of 52F at center and 45F outside and a date of 4/6
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. -Employee was found making naan dough with bowl positioned in trash can.
Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. -Raw chicken was found stored above cooked potatoes.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -No sanitation bucket present in kitchen with two in-use cloths stored on cutting board. -Server sanitation bucket found with less than 150ppm Quat solution.

Cooling of potentially hazardous food. — --Cooling of potentially hazardous food was not accomplished using proper methods. -Large bin of food was not cooled properly. Dated 4/6 and temperature was still around 50F with a walk-in cooler temp of 38-40F.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -HVAC in kitchen has tape/cardboard to block air vents.

Clean-in-place (CIP) equipment - Design and construction. — ---Door handle at side door (leads to walk-in cooler) has a sticky residue built up. -Ovens have an excess of carbon build up and needs cleaned. -3 sink dish area needs attention with dirty dishes/bins stacked in all three compartments. -Greasy walls/floors throughout the kitchen.--Nonfood contact surface of clean in place equipment was not cleaned properly. -Reach in freezer bottom has a large amount of crumbs and needs cleaned.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Cold-hold prep table has standing water that food storage containers are sitting in. Prep table needs cleaning--Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -In-use serving utensils from the cold-hold prep table have heavy build up of sauce/food product on handle.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required.
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. -Plastic Pitcher found in hand sink basin.
Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder or bar soap.
Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Carpeting was installed as floor covering where not allowed. -Carpet used in walk-in cooler.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Large amount of grease build-up behind grill and on walls.

General premises. — --Food establishment and all parts of the property in connection with their operation were not kept free from litter. -Empty cardboard, trash, and discarded jug of garlic were stored by side door.
Food service manager certification. — --Person in charge did not hold an approved food service manager certificate. -Person in charge has a food handler's certificated Issued January 3, 2026

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. -Heavy Cream left out throughout lunch and observed at 50.5F -Minced Garlic was prepped and left out throughout lunch and observed at 63F. -Shredded Cheese was left out during service.

Person in charge. — --Certified food service manager had an expired certificate or is no longer associated with this establishment.-Owner's CFSM certificate expired 2024 and now has a food handler certificate posted that was received May 21, 2025. *Owner has opened other kitchens (8 total now) throughout other states and is only on-site approximately once per month.

Food supplies. — --Food item was adulterated, contaminated or unsafe for human consumption. -Raw lamb roasts were placed into prep sink full of water last night. Found today with internal temperature of 54F. *Disposed of immediately.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. -Chicken on sheet pan on top of trash can next to hand sink uncovered.--Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -Case of potatoes stored on floor below dry storage shelf.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. -Knife stuck in crack between prep table and hand sink. -Different knife stuck between top door and side of prep table. -Tongs stored on handles of oven.

Cooling of potentially hazardous food. — --Cooked potentially hazardous food was not cooled properly from 70F to 41F or below within four hours. -Rice made last night found in walk in cooler with temperature of 45F-47F. *Directed to dispose of product.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Majority of items were not date marked, i.e. soups in bottom of prep table, open thawed bag of pastries in glass door cooler, bulk made sauce in walk in cooler.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable.-Kitchen Entrance curtains are not smooth, cleanable or nonabsorbent.

General equipment and utensils - Use limitations of certain materials. — --Material of food contact equipment/utensil is not approved for use by food service code.-White grocery bags being used in direct contact storage of raw meats in 2 door stainless freezer.

Food-contact surfaces - Design and construction. — --Multiuse food-contact surfaces did not meet design and/or construction requirements.-Bowl left as scoop in pan of sauce in walk in cooler. -Bowl left as scoop in container of dry rice.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition.-Plastic table near dish pit is sagging and worn. -Right oven door does not close or open properly due to a broken hinge.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required.-Knife at cutting board was left dirty from yesterday's service.-Tongs at ovens are visible soiled-left dirty from yesterday's service.-Commercial can opener has heavy build up of residue/debris.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required.-Surfaces throughout kitchen have build up of food debris/residue, i.e. splatter at front of ice machine, debris on tops of buckets at dishwasher, prep table handles and fronts, staining at entrance curtains, residue/debris at food storage container lids and exteriors, crumbs in bottom of 2 door stainless freezer, residue at walk in cooler shelves, etc.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Employee observed washing hands in a ware wash sink. -Both kitchen employees came inside from break and washed their hands at the 3 comp sink.--Sanitizer solution for three compartment sink was not maintained at the proper concentration.-Sani Sink found with less than 200ppm Quat solution. *Residue/limescale build up at wash sink.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical ware washing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. -Dishwasher found with 25ppm chlorine solution. *Staff stated dishes are done at 3 comp sink, however, a tray full of silverware was in the dishwasher. *Dishwasher has build up of limescale/debris at edges and surfacse.
Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees.-Water pitcher in hand sink basin upon arrive with access impeded by a pan of chicken.
Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Carpet rug being used in walk in cooler.

General premises. — --Food establishment and all parts of the property in connection with their operation were not kept free from litter. -Empty cardboard and trash is being stored outside near side entrance and walk in cooler area.

Use of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not used and/or applied according to manufacturer's label. -Staff added Quat tabs to sink and then added liquid bleach to sani sink.
Method, clean, temp, concentration, exposure time, equipment/utensils sanitized (4 pts deducted)
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Available, knowledgeable, certified manager (4 pts deducted)
Storage, properly handled (1 pt deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pt deducted)
Method, clean, temp, concentration, exposure time, equipment/utensils sanitized (4 pts deducted)
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Number, location, accessibility, soap and paper towels provided (3 pts deducted)
Available, knowledgeable, certified manager (4 pts deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Storage, properly handled (1 pt deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pt deducted)
Properly designed, located, constructed, operated, maintained, cleaned and chemical test kit provided (2 pts deducted)
